meaning: n.基督被钉死之地(位于Jerusalem附近),受难的场所,墓地
examples: Golgotha. We've got Marketing bottled up on the second floor.
They came to a place called Golgotha (which means the place of the Skull).
They have the fate to cross over the pass of anguish, the Golgotha of tears.
The digital version includes the map of Golgotha, but something is lost in the translation, I think.
Not only he, but the whole of Russia, was suffering a Golgotha from which resurrection would come.
